papers.title: May I have your attention - Designers' issue selling processes in design integration
papers.abstract: This study examines how designers engage in issue selling—the process by which organizational members direct attention to particular issues to influence strategic decisions—to promote design integration within firms. Drawing on qualitative interviews and ethnographic observation of designers across multiple organizations, the research identifies key issue selling tactics used by designers to gain organizational attention for design, including prototyping for visibility, translating design language into management language, strategic timing, and coalition building. Findings reveal that the effectiveness of these tactics depends on organizational context, design unit position, and relationships with senior management.
papers.summary_ja: デザイナーが組織内で「イシューセリング（上位者の注意をデザインに向けて戦略的意思決定に影響する行為）」を通じてデザイン統合を推進するプロセスと戦術を実証的に解明した研究。プロトタイプによる可視化・経営言語へのフレーミング翻訳・タイミング設定・連帯形成という4戦術類型を特定し、デザイン部門の組織的プレゼンス向上の実践知を提供。

papers.title: University–industry collaboration for AI-driven service innovation
papers.abstract: Service innovation is undergoing a fundamental transformation due to artificial intelligence. This study examines how universities and industry firms collaborate to drive AI-driven service innovation, identifying the structures, processes, and value co-creation mechanisms that underpin successful partnerships. Drawing on qualitative case studies, the research maps a three-phase collaboration model (exploratory, experimental, implementation) and identifies key boundary-spanning activities that enable knowledge transfer between academic and industrial partners.
papers.summary_ja: AI駆動のサービスイノベーションにおける大学-産業連携の構造・プロセス・価値共創メカニズムを実証的に解明し、探索・実験・実装の3段階連携モデルと「境界活動」の重要性を明らかにした研究。B2BサービスのAI化ロードマップ設計に直結する知見を提供。

papers.title: Syncing Minds and Machines: Hybrid Cognitive Alignment as an Emergent Coordination Mechanism in Human–AI Collaboration
papers.abstract: As humans and AI increasingly work together in organizations, how can they dynamically allocate tasks amid evolving task demands? This paper introduces the concept of Hybrid Cognitive Alignment (HCA) as an emergent coordination mechanism in human–AI collaboration. HCA refers to the process by which humans and AI dynamically adjust their cognitive roles, responsibilities, and representations to achieve effective collaborative task performance. We theorize three constitutive mechanisms: cognitive mapping, dynamic task reallocation, and shared representation building. We discuss implications for organizational design, management practice, and future research on human-AI collaboration.
papers.summary_ja: 人間とAIが組織内で協働する際に変化するタスク要求に応じて認知的役割分担を動態的に調整する「ハイブリッド認知アラインメント（HCA）」を提唱。認知マッピング・動態的タスク再配分・共有表現構築の3メカニズムから成る理論的枠組みを構築し、AI時代の組織設計に新たな理論基盤を提供した論文。

papers.title: Why Industrial Internet of Things Platforms Fail: A Structuration Theory Perspective on Platform Evolution
papers.abstract: Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) platforms have emerged as a key strategic asset for manufacturing firms, yet many fail to achieve sustainable growth. Drawing on Giddens' structuration theory, this paper examines the mechanisms through which IIoT platforms fail, focusing on the interplay between platform structure and human agency in platform evolution. Through multiple case studies of IIoT platform ventures, the study identifies structural lock-in as a primary failure mechanism and proposes conditions for maintaining adaptive flexibility.
papers.summary_ja: 産業用IoT（IIoT）プラットフォームの失敗原因をGiddensの構造化理論を援用して実証的に解明。プラットフォーム構造と人間エージェンシーの相互作用から生じる「構造的固着（structural lock-in）」を主要な失敗メカニズムとして特定し、適応的柔軟性を維持するための条件を提示したB2B製造業に直結する研究。

papers.title: Digitalization strategy in service ecosystems: managing the interplay of tensions and empowerment
papers.abstract: This paper examines how organizations develop and implement digitalization strategies in service ecosystems, focusing on the tensions that arise between efficiency and empowerment. The study proposes a framework for managing the interplay between digital transformation imperatives and the empowerment of actors within service ecosystems, drawing on paradox theory and service-dominant logic.
papers.summary_ja: サービスエコシステムのデジタル化戦略において発生する効率化・標準化・集権化と、エンパワーメント・カスタマイズ・分権化の間の多層的テンションをパラドックス理論とSDLを援用して管理するフレームワークを提示した概念研究。DX推進の実践的羅針盤となる。

papers.title: Design principles of dialogue about science and technology and the design frictions they reveal: Towards a demo-technical analysis
papers.abstract: Dialogue about science and technology is a public practice whose design is contested by multiple social actors. This paper examines the design principles underlying science and technology dialogue initiatives and the design frictions that emerge when these principles conflict. Drawing on Science and Technology Studies (STS) and design research, we propose a demo-technical analysis framework that integrates democratic deliberation and technical mediation perspectives to understand how dialogues about science and technology are designed, enacted, and contested.
papers.summary_ja: 科学・技術に関する公的対話の「設計原理」を複数の社会的アクターが競合する場として捉え、STS（科学技術社会論）とデザイン研究を統合した「デモ技術的分析」フレームワークを提唱。参加型デザインと科学コミュニケーションを架橋し、ステークホルダー対話設計に新たな分析視角を提供。

papers.title: Moving forward while swaying left and right: How to govern national innovation systems-catalysed innovation ecosystems?
papers.abstract: There is a burgeoning literature on innovation ecosystems and their governance, but questions remain about how national innovation systems (NIS) catalyse and govern such ecosystems. This paper examines the governance challenges that arise when national innovation systems activate innovation ecosystems, proposing a framework for navigating institutional and political tensions (swaying) while maintaining forward momentum in ecosystem development.
papers.summary_ja: 国家イノベーションシステム（NIS）が触媒となって形成されるイノベーションエコシステムのガバナンス課題を分析し、制度的・政治的「振れ（swaying）」を乗り越えながらエコシステムを前進させる統治モデルを提示。産官学連携型イノベーションの設計・推進に有用な枠組み。

papers.title: Are We Conflating Innovation Resistance With Innovation (Non‐)Adoption? A Reflection and Potential Resolution
papers.abstract: In the realm of innovation, academic researchers and managers often conflate innovation resistance with innovation non-adoption, treating them as interchangeable concepts. This paper reflects on this conflation and proposes a resolution by theoretically distinguishing the two concepts. Innovation resistance refers to an active, motivated opposition to adopting an innovation, while non-adoption describes a passive absence of adoption behaviour. We argue that distinguishing these concepts has significant implications for how firms design interventions to promote innovation adoption.
papers.summary_ja: イノベーション研究で混用されてきた「イノベーション抵抗（能動的拒否）」と「イノベーション不採用（受動的非選択）」を概念的に峻別し、それぞれに対応した採用促進介入の設計精緻化を提唱。新製品・新サービス導入の障壁分析と対策立案に直結する理論的整理。

papers.title: Unlocking firm innovation under regional disadvantage: Can psychological openness compensate?
papers.abstract: Firms located in regions with weak innovation systems face structural disadvantages in accessing knowledge, talent, and collaboration partners. This study examines whether psychological openness—a firm-level disposition toward seeking and embracing external knowledge and diverse perspectives—can compensate for regional disadvantage. Drawing on large-scale survey data, the study finds that psychological openness is positively associated with innovation activity and that this effect is stronger in disadvantaged regions, suggesting that mindset can partially compensate for geographic constraints.
papers.summary_ja: イノベーションインフラが脆弱な地域に立地する企業でも、「心理的開放性（外部知識・多様な視点への志向）」が高い場合にはイノベーション活動が活発化し、地理的ハンディを部分的に補償できることを大規模調査で実証。マインドセット形成とオープンイノベーション文化構築の重要性を示す。

papers.title: Super apps as catalysts: designing value constellations in open and digital ecosystems
papers.abstract: Super apps—multipurpose mobile platforms that integrate diverse services into a unified interface—are increasingly recognized as catalysts for open and digital ecosystems. This paper examines how super apps design value constellations: networks of co-created value across multiple actors in an ecosystem. Drawing on value constellation theory and platform strategy literature, the study develops a framework for understanding how super apps orchestrate ecosystem actors, design service integration architectures, and catalyse network effects.
papers.summary_ja: WeChat・Grab・Kakaoなどのスーパーアプリが複数のエコシステムアクター間の価値共創ネットワーク「価値星座（Value Constellations）」を設計・触媒するメカニズムを分析。価値星座論（Normann & Ramirez）をデジタルプラットフォーム文脈に更新し、スーパーアプリ化を目指す企業の戦略設計に有用な新フレームを提示。
